bullets: Ranked #2 in the tournament model with a 1.868 SG Total, powered by elite off-the-tee play (0.623 SG OTT).
Finished Top 5 at The Open Championship, carrying real form into this week.
TPC Twin Cities rewards ball-striking, and his 0.618 SG Around-the-Green mark is a clear edge here.
outrights.risk: Missed the cut at the Scottish Open the week before, a rare recent blemish.

bullets: Leads the tournament model at 2.002 SG Total, driven by a standout 1.285 SG Putting week-over-week average.
Course fit profile matches TPC Twin Cities' scoring demands.
Missed cuts at both the Open and Scottish Open, so this is a value price relative to model rank if the odds reflect that recent skid.
outrights.risk: Two straight missed cuts is a real red flag on current form despite the model rank.
angles: Missed Scottish Open cut -- workload without results

bullets: Model rank #2 with elite SG OTT.
Top-5 finish at The Open shows current form.
Strong approach play (0.635 SG APP) gives a reliable tee-to-green floor for a top-10 bet.
Finished 11-20 at The Open -- solid, not spectacular, but shows he's playing well.
A top-10 bet carries less variance than an outright at this price point.
top10.risk: Skipped the Scottish Open, so there's a two-week layoff to monitor for sharpness.

bullets: Model rank #3 with a strong 0.474 SG Approach mark supports a reliable top-20 floor.
Skipped The Open but made the cut at the Scottish Open, showing competitive sharpness without major fatigue.
FedExCup bubble status (rank #52) adds motivation to bank points this week.
top20.risk: Did not play a major this cycle, so there's less high-pressure form data to lean on.

preview.tournamentOverview: The 3M Open returns to TPC Twin Cities, a par-71 track that rewards accurate iron play over raw length. It's the first full-field event after The Open Championship, drawing a mix of players fresh off Royal Birkdale and others who used the week to rest.
preview.modelExplainer: This week's course weights lean heavily on SG Approach (25.8%) and SG Total (22.8%), with SG Off-the-Tee and Around-the-Green playing secondary roles. Driving accuracy and bogey avoidance carry only marginal weight, reflecting a course that punishes poor iron play more than a stray tee shot.
preview.pickApproach: With this being the week immediately after The Open, we're weighing recent workload and Open/Scottish Open results alongside the base model rather than ignoring them. Outright plays lean on current-form evidence; top-10 and top-20 plays lean on floor stats and FedExCup motivation.

article.title: 3M Open Betting Preview: Post-Open Angles, Model Picks, and Value at TPC Twin Cities
article.dek: Scottie Scheffler's Open form, a wave of Scottish Open workload, and FedExCup bubble motivation shape this week's board.
article.introduction: The tour heads to TPC Twin Cities for the 3M Open, the first stop after The Open Championship. This week's picks weigh both the course-weighted model and the workload/motivation angles that come with a major hangover week.

sections.heading: The Tournament
sections.body: TPC Twin Cities is a approach-first track, and this week's model weights SG Approach above every other category at 25.8%.

sections.heading: Post-Open Workload and Motivation
sections.body: Scottie Scheffler carries a Top 5 finish at The Open into this week on 6 competitive rounds across two weeks. Jake Knapp, by contrast, missed the cut at both the Open and the Scottish Open -- two missed cuts worth watching before leaning on his model rank.

sections.heading: Outright Targets
sections.body: Scottie Scheffler is the clear top target given his Open form and model rank #2. Jake Knapp is the value play at #1 in the model, at a price that may not fully reflect his recent form dip.

sections.heading: Top-10 Targets
sections.body: Hideki Matsuyama's approach numbers and his rested week after skipping the Scottish Open make him a strong top-10 target with lower variance than an outright bet.

sections.heading: Top-20 Targets
sections.body: Sudarshan Yellamaraju profiles as a high-floor top-20 play, with FedExCup bubble status (rank #52) adding real motivation to bank points this week.

sections.heading: Players to Approach Cautiously
sections.body: Jake Knapp's model rank looks strong, but two consecutive missed cuts across the Open and Scottish Open are a real form concern worth weighing against the price.

article.conclusion: Strongest play: Scottie Scheffler outright. Secondary value: Jake Knapp outright, Hideki Matsuyama top 10. Floor play: Sudarshan Yellamaraju top 20.
